summary: Possible typosquat of ulid/ulidx with identity mismatch and bloated runtime dependencies
details: Package is published as 'ulid-xyz' but the bundled README instructs users to run 'npm install index-ulid --save' and 'import { ulid } from "index-ulid"'. The shipped code is a copy of the legitimate ulid/ulidx library and contains no exfiltration, dropper, install-time fetch+execute, silent-relay, or credential-handling logic in this version. The name is within a small edit distance of the well-known 'ulid' and 'ulidx' packages, and the README points at yet a third name ('index-ulid'), suggesting the tarball was prepared as one of several near-identical sibling packages. Additionally, package.json declares unrelated runtime dependencies — pino, ws, zod, postinstall, esbuild, ts-node, tsup, @types/node, @types/ws — that the shipped dist/ code does not use (only node:crypto is required). Misplacing dev tooling and unrelated libraries into runtime dependencies expands install-time surface and is a common precursor to staged supply-chain attacks in later versions, but no installer-harm behavior is present in 3.1.0 itself.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
